Gene_Accession_Number.reason: At5g35730 is explicitly identified as ERD1A in Results, and NP_568530.1 appears in the Accession numbers section.

Source_Species.reason: ERD1A is an Arabidopsis gene throughout the paper.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper explicitly states Arabidopsis (Arabidopsis thaliana).

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Methods state all Arabidopsis plants used were in the Columbia (Col-0) background.

Applied_Species.reason: ERD1A was directly tested in Arabidopsis.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The applied plant system is Arabidopsis thaliana.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Arabidopsis experiments were done in Col-0 background.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The study directly measures phosphate (Pi) export and identifies ERD1A as involved in Golgi Pi homeostasis.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Pi is a mineral nutrient; this classification is consistent with the paper's framing of phosphate homeostasis.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Results explicitly show increased Pi export to the apoplast in erd1a, and the paper discusses Golgi Pi export/retention context.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Abstract and Results link Golgi Pi homeostasis to nucleotide-sugar transport, cell wall synthesis/protein glycosylation, growth, and fertility.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: ERD1A is explicitly described as a protein possessing an EXS domain; the paper functionally identifies ERD1 proteins as Golgi Pi exporters.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: GUS data show broad ERD1A expression in root meristem, vascular tissues, leaves, stamen filament, pistil, and pollen; RT-qPCR/RNA-seq show higher expression than ERD1B in roots/leaves.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: This matches the Arabidopsis expression evidence reported for ERD1A, including broader/higher expression than ERD1B in roots and shoots/leaves.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The paper uses the native ERD1A promoter for complementation/GUS and CaMV35S for overexpression constructs.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: All listed factors are directly tested: BFA abolished increased Pi export, dark-grown hypocotyls were assayed, and increased external Pi did not rescue the phenotype.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results directly support increased apoplastic Pi export in erd1a and defects in cell wall composition, hypocotyl/root growth, pollen germination, pollen tube growth, and fertility.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each point is supported by Results/Abstract: cis-Golgi localization, elevated apoplastic Pi export, lack of strong whole-plant Pi starvation signatures, altered wall composition/growth/fertility, complementation by native promoter, and overexpression-associated PM mislocalization/Pi export.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are explicitly used in Results and Methods for ERD1A.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is an extrapolated application statement; the paper does not directly test breeding/engineering utility, though it does show manipulation has strong developmental consequences.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Knockout stunting and male sterility are directly shown; overexpression-associated PM mislocalization and increased apoplastic Pi export are directly shown.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: ManI and CBL1 are localization markers showing colocalization, and ERD1A/ERD1B co-localize, but the paper does not demonstrate physical protein-protein interactions.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: ERD1A localizes to the cis-Golgi, and strong overexpression causes partial plasma membrane localization.

Omics_Data.reason: The Results explicitly cite published RNA-seq data from Arabidopsis roots and leaves (Deforges et al. 2019; Supplemental Table S1) for expression comparison; no accession is provided in the paper.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results support distinction from PHT4;6 and the proposed interpretation of overexpression-induced PM mislocalization versus mutant-associated vesicular Pi export is presented in the paper.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: At2g32295 is explicitly renamed ERD1B in Results, and NP_001318333.1 appears in the Accession numbers section.

Source_Species.reason: ERD1B is an Arabidopsis gene in this study.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper explicitly states Arabidopsis thaliana.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Methods specify all Arabidopsis plants were in the Col-0 background.

Applied_Species.reason: ERD1B was tested in Arabidopsis for expression/complementation/localization.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Arabidopsis thaliana is the applied plant species.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: The Arabidopsis background used was Col-0.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: ERD1B complements the yeast erd1 Pi-export phenotype and is concluded to have similar function to ERD1A in Golgi Pi homeostasis.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The paper supports Golgi Pi export context and apoplastic/export phenotypes in the ERD1 system, though Arabidopsis ERD1B-specific apoplastic export was not directly assayed alone.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The study links ERD1 proteins to Golgi Pi homeostasis required for glycosylation/cell wall biosynthesis and reproductive function; ERD1B is inferred to share this function by complementation.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: ERD1B is explicitly one of two Arabidopsis proteins possessing an EXS domain and is functionally grouped with ERD1 phosphate exporters.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: GUS data show broad but weaker ERD1B expression in cotyledon, root, rosette leaf, and pistil, with no staining in stamen/pollen under the reported conditions.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Results and RNA-seq/RT-qPCR support lower ERD1B expression than ERD1A in roots and leaves and generally weaker broad expression.

Applied_Promoters.reason: ERD1B was analyzed under its native promoter, the ERD1A promoter, and CaMV35S promoter.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show erd1b single mutants lacked visible phenotype, while ERD1B could complement erd1a when expressed sufficiently, indicating similar function.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All listed findings are supported: close homolog/cis-Golgi localization, no obvious single-mutant phenotype, promoter-dependent complementation of erd1a, and requirement together with ERD1A for male transmission.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods were used for ERD1B directly in the paper.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is speculative application language not directly tested in the paper.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper directly supports abolished male transmission in the double-deficient background and lower expression/accumulation of ERD1B unless strongly expressed.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: ERD1A and ManI are shown as co-localization comparators/markers, not proven interacting proteins.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: ERD1B is shown to co-localize with the cis-Golgi marker and not with the TGN marker.

Omics_Data.reason: Published RNA-seq data from roots and leaves were explicitly used to compare ERD1A and ERD1B expression; no accession is given.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results support lower ERD1B transgene accumulation than analogous ERD1A constructs and lack of double-mutant male transmission.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: The Accession numbers section lists Saccharomyces cerevisiae NP_010702.4, and Methods identify the yeast erd1 mutant as YDR414c::kanMX4.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The source species is explicitly Saccharomyces cerevisiae.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Methods specify the yeast strain BY4742 and the erd1 mutant derived from it.

Applied_Species.reason: ScERD1 was directly tested in yeast.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The applied species is Saccharomyces cerevisiae.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: The experiments use the BY4742 background and corresponding erd1 mutant.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Yeast assays directly measure Pi export and the paper discusses ScERD1 in Golgi Pi homeostasis.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Results explicitly measure increased Pi export to the external medium/apoplastic space in yeast and discuss Golgi context.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The study links ScERD1-mediated Pi homeostasis to cell wall mannan biosynthesis in yeast.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: ScERD1 is the fungal ERD1 ortholog aligned with Arabidopsis EXS-domain proteins and part of the ERD1 family discussed in the paper.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Results/Methods describe expression under the native ScERD1 promoter and strong GPD (TDH3) promoter in p415/p425 vectors.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Low-Pi medium is explicitly used in the yeast Pi export assay.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show ScERD1 complements yeast erd1 defects in Alcian blue staining, mannan content, and excess Pi export; overexpression in WT increases Pi export.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three listed points are directly supported by Fig. 4 and associated text.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are explicitly used for ScERD1 in the yeast experiments.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is interpretive and not directly addressed experimentally.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper directly shows that ScERD1 overexpression elevates extracellular Pi export in WT yeast.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: The Discussion states the S. cerevisiae ERD1 ortholog resides in cis-Golgi citing other studies, but this paper itself does not directly test ScERD1 localization in Results.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results directly show Arabidopsis ERD1A/B complement yeast erd1 Pi-export and cell-wall phenotypes, supporting conserved function.
